behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: Failure is most likely at the handoff between history-management logic and the LLM request builder. A pure unit test of a trimming helper can miss wiring/order bugs in the actual payload sent to the model. No real OS/network/LLM-quality dependency is required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire chatbot request handling with real prompt-construction modules and a test LLM client stub that records the outbound payload. Provide a history longer than 10 messages, execute one chat turn, and assert the LLM call contains exactly the latest 10 messages in correct order (older messages excluded).

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: This is response-quality correctness, which depends on real model behavior and can vary across runs/prompts. Mocked model outputs would hide the actual failure mode (generic/off-topic generations).
behaviors.test_description: Run a real-model evaluation set of customer questions with expected topical intent. For each run, score relevance (rubric/semantic checks) and fail when responses are generic or off-topic beyond threshold. Repeat enough samples/seeds to catch variance-driven regressions.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: The core claim is correctness of tone identification (formal vs casual), which is model-judgment dependent if implemented via LLM. Mocks can verify trigger wiring but cannot validate real tone classification reliability.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative too-formal responses for casual-tier context through the real tone-check path and verify regeneration is triggered. Include borderline samples and repeated runs to detect misclassification variance; assert regeneration call occurs when flagged.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: This is a multi-module persistence boundary (chat flow + repository/ORM + DB schema). Unit tests won't catch mapping/transaction/timestamp write issues. A local test DB is sufficient; no real external infra is required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a chat turn (including a forced regeneration path) against a real local test database. Assert stored records include user message, initial draft, regenerated draft/final response, and expected timestamp fields with correct ordering and values.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at orchestration/output boundary: tone-check decision, regeneration call, and final response selection. This can be caught without real model quality checks by controlling module outputs; pure unit tests may miss handoff bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Run chatbot flow with deterministic stubs: first response is flagged, second response is accepted. Assert API/output channel returns only the second response and that the rejected draft is never emitted to the customer-facing response object/stream.
